title: referrals — marketing events (ad-network conversions)
description: Marketing-lane events fired by the referrals module. Every referral that reaches signed_up + vested transitions its acquisition path to a first-class attributable conversion — the marketing module forwards these to configured ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, Microsoft UET, Snapchat CAPI) using the frozen attribution_snapshot's click_id + utm data. Marketing events are ZERO-LOSS — each lost event = wasted ad spend + broken attribution. Consent tier is `advertising` (subset of `marketing`) — a user who revokes advertising consent has their conversions suppressed at dispatch (attribution snapshot is not re-shared with ad networks).

events.name: Referral Signup Attributed
events.description: Fires on ReferralSignedUp. Attributes the signup to the ad-network that seeded the referrer's original acquisition (or the referred user's own attribution path when known).

events.name: Referral Vested Attributed
properties: value_cents (revenue attribution — often the first_payment amount if trigger=first_payment)
events.description: Fires on ReferralVested. The HIGH-VALUE conversion — this is what ad-network bidding algorithms optimise against. Includes revenue attribution (value_cents) when trigger_event=first_payment.

events.name: Referral Signup Reversed
events.description: Fires on ReferralCancelled (when the underlying referral had already fired a Signup Attributed event). Ad networks receive a reversal signal so they can adjust bidding — critical for accurate ROAS calculation. Not all networks support reversal — the ones that do get the reversal event; the others receive a compensating negative-value conversion on providers that support it.

events.name: Referral Purchase Reversed
events.description: Fires on ReferralClawback (when the underlying reward had already fired a Vested Attributed event). Reverses the ad-network purchase conversion — protects downstream ROAS + fraud-model training data.

events.name: Referral Invitation Delivered
events.description: Fires on ReferralInvitationSent. Lower-tier engagement event — only fanned out to Meta CAPI (for lookalike audience building). Other networks ignore.

delivery_semantics.retry_policy: exponential backoff (10s / 60s / 5m / 30m / 3h / 24h) up to 6 attempts; then dead-letter queue for manual replay
delivery_semantics.circuit_breaker: per-provider circuit breaker in the marketing module; if one provider is down, the others still receive events
delivery_semantics.ledger: growth::marketing owns the two-table ledger: marketing_events (canonical) + marketing_deliveries (per-provider-per-attempt log)

pii_hashing.user_id: sent as external_id (unhashed) — matches ad-network's advanced-matching contract

notes: This is the MARKETING lane per growth-and-observability.md — different consent tier (`advertising`, subset of marketing) than the analytics lane (`functional`) even for the same underlying event.
Ad networks require SHA-256 hashing of PII per their contracts — the growth::marketing module handles hashing, this module just declares which properties are PII.
Reversal events are critical — without them, ROAS calculations inflate and the ad network keeps bidding on false conversions.
attribution_snapshot_shape_version is bumped when the frozen snapshot shape changes — downstream marketing forwarders can branch on the version to handle evolution.
